campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and vote on changes to business license tax rates in the city. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented the annual update to these rates, which would be updated in the municipal code and take effect in the new fiscal year. The council held a public discussion and made a decision on whether to approve the rate changes.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being updated for the new fiscal year
Changes require amending the Campbell Municipal Code
Finance Department presented the specific rate proposals
Council held public discussion before voting on the changes

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the standard first reading process, with plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on proposed ordinance
First reading waived to speed up approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous sessions and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized several construction and professional service contracts to move forward with various town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7 closed and public sessions
Reviewed city's monthly financial and investment report for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with multiple vendors including QLM, Inc.
Approved three-year professional services agreement (details not fully listed)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 22, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for late April and early May 2026. The council reviewed what items should be added to their official meeting schedules and determined which topics need dedicated time slots to ensure proper discussion.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 28, 2026 council meeting agenda
Set up the May 5, 2026 draft agenda with new items
Assigned time slots to specific agenda items for efficient meetings
Reviewed legal notice requirements for agenda items and documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a meeting to review Mountain View's spending plans for the next fiscal year, focusing on capital improvement projects like infrastructure and facilities. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Budget review for 2026-27 capital projects (roads, buildings, public facilities)
Teen Month proclamation honoring young residents
National Volunteer Week recognition for community volunteers

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a closed-door meeting at 6 PM to discuss confidential matters (the specific topics weren't made public), followed by a regular council meeting at 7 PM where they recognized and honored award winners in the community.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not disclosed to public)
Community recognition and award winners ceremony
Regular council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 21, 2026 to discuss two ongoing legal cases that could potentially cost the city money, and to recognize the work done by the Administrative Services Department (the teams that handle the city's budget, finances, and purchasing). The meeting also included time for residents to speak about any city matters they wanted to bring up.
keyTopics: Two legal cases with potential financial risk to the city
Recognition of Budget, Finance, and Purchasing Divisions
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large graphic signs to be put on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events, and to approve a new employment agreement with firefighters for 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted around Levi's Stadium during World Cup 2026
New three-year contract approved for Santa Clara firefighters
Staff withdrew one agenda item before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to approve the city's next fiscal year budget and various fees and services, review plans to address homelessness in the county, and handle routine meeting business like approving previous minutes.
keyTopics: Approved fiscal year 2026-27 budget and development fees
Reissued Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act requirements
Updated city user fees and service charges
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan presentation
Approved previous meeting minutes and scheduled future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a previously approved permit to remove a protected tree at 751 Waverley Street. Someone asked the Planning Director to take another look at the decision that was already made through the standard planning process.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street
Request to have Planning Director reconsider an already-approved decision
Fast-track 'over-the-counter' planning process being reviewed
